channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: DEI rollbacks hit campus support systems for students of color
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Campus mentors. Move-in events. Scholarships. Diversity offices that made them feel welcome on predominantly white campuses. As U.S. colleges pull back on diversity, equity and inclusion practices, students of color say they are starting to lose all of these things and more.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Orange Crush 2025: Georgia vacation town braces for mayhem as crime-filled beach fest returns
item.description: Orange Crush is expected to draw tens of thousands of people to Tybee Island, Georgia, this Easter weekend. Event organizers promise to keep the event safe and clean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court blocks, for now, new deportations under 18th century wartime law
item.description: The Supreme Court on Saturday blocked, for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania's presidential election redo is a high-stakes test of its democracy following annulled vote
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania's presidential election redo next month will be a high-stakes test for the European Union member’s democracy after last year’s annulled vote triggered the country’s deepest political crisis since 1989. Romania’s Constitutional Court annulled the election on Dec.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and NICOLAE DUMITRACHE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration makes major cuts to Native American boarding school research projects
item.description: At least $1.6 million in federal funds for projects meant to capture and digitize stories of the systemic abuse of generations of Indigenous children in boarding schools at the hands of the U.S. government have been slashed due to federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Trump backed away from promising to end the Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: During his campaign, Donald Trump said repeatedly that he would be able to end the war between Russia and Ukraine “in 24 hours” upon taking office. He has changed his tone since becoming president again. As various U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump’s first quarter approval rating sits at 45 percent: Poll
item.description: Less than half of U.S. adults say they have confidence in the president to recommend or do the right thing for the economy, according to the Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: DA Hochman denies bias against Menendez brothers
item.description: The Los Angeles County district attorney told NewsNation he's not biased against Erik and Lyle Menendez.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: US senator returns from El Salvador trip, says Abrego Garcia case is about far more than one man
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The dispute over the wrongful deportation and imprisonment of Kilmar Abrego Garcia “is not only about one man” but about Donald Trump's disregard of the American judicial system as well, Sen.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FSU shooting victims include a school employee whose dad was a Cuban exile turned CIA operative
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Two people were killed and six others were injured when a gunman opened fire at Florida State University, sending students fleeing from the student union and putting the Tallahassee, Florida, campus under lockdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Sen. Cornyn asks Trump for more funds for flood-stricken South Texas
item.description: Republican Sen. John Cornyn, of Texas, sent a letter Friday to President Trump asking for more help for flood-stricken South Texas, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Montana reports first measles cases since 1990
item.description: Two children have died amid the outbreak this year and a third death is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) and other conservative allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC).
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KRON4

item.title: 10,000 pages of records about Robert F. Kennedy's 1968 assassination are released, on Trump's order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — About 10,000 pages of records related to the 1968 assassination of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy were released Friday, including handwritten notes by the gunman, who said the Democratic presidential candidate “must be disposed of” and acknowledged an obsession with killing him.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and HAYA PANJWANI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge to allow 'bushy eyebrows' testimony in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — A judge says a former roommate of four University of Idaho students who were killed in 2022 can testify about seeing an intruder with “bushy eyebrows” around the time of the crime.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apartments are getting larger -- but maybe not where you live
item.description: RentCafe’s annual report on apartment living shows some communities are seeing apartment square footage increase for the first time in years. But maybe not yours.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wilgoren, KTLA 5

item.title: Idaho judge denies Bryan Kohberger's 'bushy eyebrows' motion
item.description: Bryan Kohberger's trial will proceed with key evidence after a judge denied a defense request to block eyewitness testimony about "bushy eyebrows" in the Idaho student murders case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana takes a first -- and long -- shot at acquiring parts of Illinois
item.description: Indiana is taking a first step toward acquiring parts of Illinois, though a last step appears rather unlikely.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US has 800 measles cases and outbreaks in several states. Here's what you should know
item.description: The U.S. has 800 cases of measles nationwide as of Friday, and two more states identified outbreaks this week. Texas is driving the high numbers, with an outbreak centered in West Texas that started nearly three months ago and is up to 597 cases.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abrego Garcia told visiting US senator he was no longer being held at notorious Salvadoran prison
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen was flying home from El Salvador on Friday after meeting with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was wrongly deported there by the Trump administration. It’s unclear what will happen next in the case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Video shows doctor with measles treating kids. RFK Jr later praised him as an ‘extraordinary’ healer
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — A Texas doctor who has been treating children in a measles outbreak was shown on video with a measles rash on his face in a clinic a week before Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. met him and praised him as an “extraordinary” healer. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google reveals California’s favorite Easter candy
item.description: Everyone has their go-to Easter treat—be it chocolate eggs, jellybeans, or gummies—but a new Google Trends study shows which sweet was the favorite in each state. Peeps were found to be Californians’ top Easter candy choice.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: More than 1,000 international students have had visas or legal status revoked
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 1,000 international students have had their visas or legal status revoked in recent weeks, and several have filed lawsuits against the Trump administration, arguing the government denied them due process when it suddenly took away their permission to be in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: 2 people killed and at least 6 wounded in Florida State shooting
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — A gunman opened fire Thursday at Florida State University, killing two people and wounding at least six others, police said. The 20-year-old gunman is the son of a sheriff’s deputy whose former service weapon was used in the shooting, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deer Valley Music Festival to feature Lindsey Stirling, Beach Boys, and Common
item.description: The 2025 Deer Valley Music Festival lineup has now been announced, featuring big names on the lineup — such as The Beach Boys, Lindsey Stirling, and Common.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, Fox 5

item.title: Jury selection heads into next week at Weinstein rape retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people were screened Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, but the selection wrapped up for the week with several slots still to be filled.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court keeps hold on Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship but sets May arguments
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday kept on hold President Donald Trump’s restrictions on birthright citizenship but agreed to hear arguments on the issue in May. Trump’s executive order to end birthright citizenship for the children of people who are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the shooting at Florida State University
item.description: Students and staff have returned to buildings on the Florida State University campus to retrieve their belongings a day after a gunman opened fire, killing two people and wounding at least six others.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two dead and 6 injured in Florida State shooting; gunman is deputy's son, police say
item.description: Authorities identified the suspected shooter as a 20-year-old student who is also the son of a local sheriff's deputy.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, KRON4

item.title: Menendez brothers hearing moves forward, albeit with confusion
item.description: The resentencing hearing for Eric and Lyle Menendez moved forward Thursday morning despite a motion for delay filed by Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WGN 9

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: NY prison guards beat an inmate to death then tried to cover it up, prosecutors say
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Newsom says California will sue over Trump tariffs
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom and Attorney General Rob Bonta announced plans to sue the Trump administration over recent tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
